minimumLength.description: The minimum number of characters required in a password.

maximumLength.description: The maximum number of characters allowed in a password.

numberRequired.description: A password must contain at least one numeric character (0-9).

symbolRequired.description: A password must contain at least one symbol (~`!@#$%^&*()_-+={[}]|\:;"'<,>.?/).

upperCaseRequired.description: A password must contain at least one uppercase letter (A-Z).

lowerCaseRequired.description: A password must contain at least one lowercase letter (a-z).

activationEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for account activation emails.

reactivationEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for account reactivation emails.

passwordResetEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for password reset emails.

oidcUnlinkEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for OIDC unlinking emails.

oidcUnlinkPinEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for OIDC unlinking PIN emails.

activationPinEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for account activation PIN emails.

reactivationPinEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for account reactivation PIN emails.

passwordResetPinEmailTemplateName.description: The name of the email template used for password reset PIN emails.

enablePinCodeActivationRequests.description: A PIN code can be used for account activation instead of an activation link.

enablePinCodeForgotPasswordRequests.description: A PIN code can be used for password reset requests instead of a password reset link.

disableForceDownloadForMimeTypes.description: All files will have a Content-Disposition header with the Attachment value when fetched, unless their MIME type matches a type in this array exactly. The Attachment value ensures files are downloaded rather than displayed directly in the browser, enhancing protection against potential XSS vulnerabilities. The value */* can be used to allow any file to be displayed by browsers, use at your own discretion.
